questions.question: 1. How do we test Buchholz relay during maintenance

answers.a: Generate air bubbles with pump and force it to Buchholz relay
answers.b: Reduce oil level in Buchholz relay

explanations.a: Partial. Air bubbles test the gas accumulation function.
explanations.b: Partial. Reducing oil level tests the float and alarm function.
explanations.c: Correct. Both methods are used to test the Buchholz relay during maintenance - air injection for gas detection and oil drain for low oil level.

questions.question: 2. Buchholz relay is used to protect from

explanations.a: Correct. Buchholz relay detects internal faults like winding short circuits, core faults, and insulation breakdown.
explanations.b: Incorrect. External faults are not detected by Buchholz relay.

questions.question: 3. Buchholz relay protects a transformer from

explanations.a: Partial. Winding to ground fault produces gas.
explanations.b: Partial. Winding to winding fault produces gas.
explanations.c: Partial. Turn to turn fault produces gas.
explanations.d: Correct. Buchholz relay detects all types of internal faults that produce gas or oil flow, including winding to ground, winding to winding, and turn to turn faults.

questions.question: 4. Minimum operating time of Buchholz relay is

explanations.a: Correct. The minimum operating time of Buchholz relay is about 0.1 second (100 milliseconds).
explanations.b: Incorrect. That is too high for minimum.

questions.question: 5. Average operating time of Buchholz relay is

explanations.a: Incorrect. That is higher than average.
explanations.b: Correct. The average operating time of Buchholz relay is approximately 0.2 seconds (200 milliseconds).
explanations.c: Incorrect. That is the minimum time, not average.
